title: Digestion with NEBNext dsDNA Fragmentase (M0348)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"3uh79","text":"NEBNext dsDNA Fragmentase is an enzyme-based reagent that shears DNA to produce fragments of the desired sizes in a time-dependent manner, for next generation sequencing library preparation protocols","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3rq1c","text":"dsDNA Fragmentase provides random fragmentation, similar to mechanical methods (1,2).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

Additional MgCl2 can be added if necessary.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":65,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":26,"length":1},{"key":2,"offset":31,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"6ergv","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"59p36","text":"The protocol listed below is for fragmentation of 5 ng–3 μg of DNA.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":50,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"duration","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"duration":3,"guid":"53A191409DAA11EA856CBF0751E31388","id":0,"label":"","pause":null,"preset":[],"start":null}},"1":{"type":"amount","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"amount":"1","guid":"5F99D6609DAA11EA856CBF0751E31388","id":0,"label":"","unit":1}},"2":{"type":"concentration","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"concentration":"200","guid":"6490E5009DAA11EA856CBF0751E31388","id":0,"label":"MgCl2","unit":28}}}}

steps: Combine the following components in a sterile PCR tube and vortex: A B 1 Component Amount 2 DNA (5 ng–3 μg) 1–16 μl 3 10X Fragmentase Reaction Buffer v2 2 μl 4 Sterile Water variable 5 Final Volume 18 μl component amount DNA (5 ng–3 μg) 1–16 μl 10X Fragmentase Reaction Buffer v2 2 μl Sterile Water variable

Add 2 µL, dsDNA Fragmentase and vortex mixture for 00:00:03 . Fragmentase is very viscous and should be pipetted slowly. If the enzyme has been sitting for several minutes vortex it again before adding to the sample.

Incubate at 37 °C for the recommended times below to generate the desired fragment size: If starting material is 100 ng or less, incubation times should be increased by 10 minutes. A B 1 Desired Fragment Size (bp) Incubation Time (min) 2 50–200 25–35 3 200–1,000 15–25 4 1,000–2,000 10–15 Desired Fragment Size (bp) Incubation Time (min) 50–200 25–35 200–1,000 15–25 1,000–2,000 10–15 To determine the exact incubation time for a given sample type, a time course study should be performed.
Add 5 µL, 0.5 M EDTA to stop the reaction.

Clean up the fragmented DNA with column purification or using SPRI beads. If using SPRI beads, it is recommended to dilute the sample 1:1 with sterile water for easier handling of the sample and faster collection of the beads to the magnet. SPRI beads are available from Beckman Coulter: A63880, A63881, A63882 Bioanalyzer: Clean up the fragmented DNA prior to loading on a Bioanalyzer chip. End Repair : Clean up the fragmented DNA then proceed with desired DNA end repair protocol. Polyacrylamide Gel Analysis: Clean up the fragmented DNA prior to loading the samples on a PAGE gel. Long Term Storage: Clean up the fragmented DNA prior to long term storage. Agarose Gel Size Selection/Analysis : Samples can be loaded directly on to an agarose gel. It is not necessary to clean up the reactions prior to loading.
Vortex NEBNext dsDNA Fragmentase for 00:00:03 , quick spin and place On ice .
creator: Isabel Gautreau
